Container.Name: unraid-cache-cleaner
Container.Overview: Polls qBittorrent and safely deletes untracked leftovers from a mounted Unraid cache/download path after a grace period.

Config.Name: Config
Config.Description: Persistent state and reports

Config.Name: Downloads
Config.Description: Mount the same path qBittorrent uses internally

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Host port mapped to the read-only Plex duplicate report web viewer (container port 8080, WEB_PORT). The viewer only listens if you set WEB_ENABLED=true or run the container with the web command; otherwise this mapping is inert.

Config.Name: QBITTORRENT_URL
Config.Description: qBittorrent WebUI URL. Use the container name on a shared Docker network or your Unraid IP and exposed port.

Config.Name: QBITTORRENT_USERNAME
Config.Description: qBittorrent WebUI username

Config.Name: QBITTORRENT_PASSWORD
Config.Description: qBittorrent WebUI password

Config.Name: WATCH_PATHS
Config.Description: Comma-separated mounted roots to scan

Config.Name: EXCLUDED_GLOBS
Config.Description: Comma-separated basename or full-path globs to skip, useful for helper scripts or log folders living under /data

Config.Name: DRY_RUN
Config.Description: Set false only after reviewing dry-run output

Config.Name: QBITTORRENT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS
Config.Description: HTTP timeout when polling qBittorrent

Config.Name: QBITTORRENT_VERIFY_TLS
Config.Description: Disable only if your qBittorrent WebUI uses a self-signed certificate

Config.Name: ORPHAN_GRACE_SECONDS
Config.Description: Seconds a file must stay orphaned before deletion

Config.Name: MIN_FILE_AGE_SECONDS
Config.Description: Minimum file age before deletion

Config.Name: POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS
Config.Description: Delay between scans in service mode

Config.Name: DELETE_EMPTY_DIRS
Config.Description: Remove empty directories after deleting orphan files

Config.Name: PROTECT_SINGLE_FILE_PARENT_DIRS
Config.Description: Protect dedicated subdirectories that contain active single-file torrents

Config.Name: STATE_DB_PATH
Config.Description: Persistent SQLite state database

Config.Name: REPORT_PATH
Config.Description: Latest JSON report written by the service

Config.Name: LOG_LEVEL
Config.Description: Python logging level

Config.Name: HTTP_MAX_ATTEMPTS
Config.Description: Attempts per idempotent (GET/HEAD) request to qBittorrent/Plex/Radarr/Sonarr. 1 = no retry (default); raise it (e.g. 3) to retry a transient 5xx or connection blip with exponential backoff. The qBittorrent login POST is never retried.

Config.Name: EXTRACT_ENABLED
Config.Description: Enable opt-in RAR extraction in every scan/service cycle (and the extract subcommand) so Radarr/Sonarr can import scene releases. Extraction mutates the download path, so it is off by default and honors DRY_RUN.

Config.Name: EXTRACT_TOOL
Config.Description: Archive binary name or full path used to extract RAR archives (lsar is derived from it for the integrity test).

Config.Name: EXTRACT_OWNER
Config.Description: Numeric uid:gid for a best-effort chown of extracted files (Unraid = 99:100). Leave empty to skip chown.

Config.Name: EXTRACT_MIN_AGE_SECONDS
Config.Description: Skip archives whose newest volume is younger than this many seconds (settle guard against still-downloading files).

Config.Name: EXTRACT_PROTECT_SECONDS
Config.Description: How long extracted output stays undeletable after extraction (default 24h), giving Radarr/Sonarr time to import before normal orphan cleanup resumes.

Config.Name: PLEX_URL
Config.Description: Plex server base URL for the read-only plex-duplicates report (e.g. http://192.168.1.10:32400). Leave empty if you only use qBittorrent cleanup.

Config.Name: PLEX_TOKEN
Config.Description: Plex X-Plex-Token for the read-only plex-duplicates report. Sent as a header, never in the URL.

Config.Name: PLEX_SECTIONS
Config.Description: Comma-separated Plex library section IDs to scan. Empty auto-detects video sections.

Config.Name: PLEX_TIMEOUT_SECONDS
Config.Description: HTTP timeout when querying Plex

Config.Name: PLEX_VERIFY_TLS
Config.Description: Disable only if your Plex server uses a self-signed certificate

Config.Name: PLEX_DUPLICATE_REPORT_PATH
Config.Description: Output path for the Plex duplicate JSON report

Config.Name: HASH_MODE
Config.Description: Optional content-hash confirmation for the plex-duplicates report: off (default, no media read), partial (size + first/last 4 MiB per copy), or full (whole-file sha256). Confirms 'identical' groups are truly byte-identical and downgrades any that are not to different-content (never reclaimable). Reads Plex paths via WEB_MEDIA_PATH_MAP, so set that map and add a read-only Path mapping for the media volume. Read-only.

Config.Name: HASH_CACHE
Config.Description: Cache content-hash digests between runs so HASH_MODE=partial/full does not re-read every media file each report. Keyed by each copy's size, mtime, ctime, and inode (any changed file is re-hashed, even a same-size/same-mtime overwrite); fail-open (a corrupt/locked cache just re-hashes, never a wrong verdict). Inert when HASH_MODE=off. Set false to always hash live.

Config.Name: HASH_CACHE_PATH
Config.Description: Location of the hash-cache SQLite sidecar (created only when the hash pass runs). Keep it under /config so the cache survives restarts.

Config.Name: RADARR_URL
Config.Description: Optional Radarr base URL (e.g. http://192.168.1.10:7878) to flag which duplicate movie copies are Radarr-tracked in the plex-duplicates report. Read-only; enabled only with RADARR_API_KEY.

Config.Name: RADARR_API_KEY
Config.Description: Radarr API key, sent as X-Api-Key (never in the URL). Required to enable Radarr tracking.

Config.Name: RADARR_TIMEOUT_SECONDS
Config.Description: HTTP timeout when querying Radarr

Config.Name: RADARR_VERIFY_TLS
Config.Description: Disable only if your Radarr uses a self-signed certificate

Config.Name: SONARR_URL
Config.Description: Optional Sonarr base URL (e.g. http://192.168.1.10:8989) to flag which duplicate episode copies are Sonarr-tracked in the plex-duplicates report. Read-only; enabled only with SONARR_API_KEY.

Config.Name: SONARR_API_KEY
Config.Description: Sonarr API key, sent as X-Api-Key (never in the URL). Required to enable Sonarr tracking.

Config.Name: SONARR_TIMEOUT_SECONDS
Config.Description: HTTP timeout when querying Sonarr

Config.Name: SONARR_VERIFY_TLS
Config.Description: Disable only if your Sonarr uses a self-signed certificate

Config.Name: WEB_ENABLED
Config.Description: Also serve the read-only Plex duplicate report web viewer from the long-running service (opt-in). Set true to light up the WebUI link on this container; leave false if you run a separate container with the web command. Off by default. Read-only: the viewer never scans or deletes.

Config.Name: WEB_BIND_ADDRESS
Config.Description: Address the web viewer binds inside the container. Keep 0.0.0.0 so the mapped host port is reachable; use 127.0.0.1 only to restrict to loopback.

Config.Name: WEB_PORT
Config.Description: Container port the web viewer listens on. Must match the WebUI Port mapping's container target.

Config.Name: WEB_ENABLE_ACTIONS
Config.Description: Enable the browser action layer so an operator can delete redundant Plex duplicate copies from the web UI. First outside-triggered deletion of library media, so it is off by default and fail-closed. Requires WEB_ACTION_TOKEN and (for untracked copies) WEB_MEDIA_PATH_MAP plus a mounted media volume.

Config.Name: WEB_ACTIONS_DRY_RUN
Config.Description: When actions are enabled, report what a reclaim would delete and touch nothing (mirrors DRY_RUN). Set false only after reviewing a dry run.

Config.Name: WEB_ACTION_TOKEN
Config.Description: Shared secret every reclaim must present. Required to delete: with actions enabled but no token, every reclaim is refused. Sent as the X-Action-Token header (JSON API), or pasted once in the browser to set an HttpOnly/SameSite unlock cookie so the two-step confirm need not re-paste it (the confirm page also carries the same signed value as a hidden field, so it works with cookies disabled). Never in the URL.

Config.Name: WEB_ACTION_SESSION_SECONDS
Config.Description: Lifetime in seconds of the browser unlock session — how long after pasting WEB_ACTION_TOKEN a confirm proceeds without re-pasting it. Drives both the cookie Max-Age and the signed-token expiry. Default 3600 (one hour); a non-positive value falls back to 3600.

Config.Name: WEB_MEDIA_PATH_MAP
Config.Description: Comma-separated plex_prefix:container_prefix pairs mapping Plex-reported paths to this container's mounts (e.g. /mnt/user/Media:/media). Needed for a filesystem delete of an untracked copy; also add a read-write Path mapping for that media volume. Tracked copies delete via Radarr/Sonarr and need no map.

Config.Name: WEB_ALLOWED_ORIGINS
Config.Description: CSRF/origin allow-list for the reclaim form when the UI is exposed beyond loopback. On the default 0.0.0.0 bind a browser form POST must be same-origin; leave this empty for direct LAN access. Only set it behind a TLS-terminating reverse proxy: a comma-separated list of external origins the browser sends, e.g. https://media.example.com. The JSON API stays token-only regardless.

Config.Name: WEB_ALLOWED_HOSTS
Config.Description: DNS-rebinding defense: comma-separated hostnames allowed in the request Host header. Every route refuses an unrecognized hostname before routing. IP-literal and localhost hosts are always accepted (an IP can't be rebound), so direct LAN access by the server IP needs no config — leave this empty. Set it only if you reach the UI through a hostname (e.g. tower.local); the hostnames of WEB_ALLOWED_ORIGINS are added automatically. X-Forwarded-* is never trusted.

Config.Name: WEB_ACTION_HISTORY_AUTH
Config.Description: Require the reclaim credential to view the read-only action-history views /actions and /api/actions (which expose previously-deleted absolute paths and sizes). Off keeps them LAN-readable like the report. When true, /api/actions needs the X-Action-Token header (or the unlock cookie) and /actions needs the browser unlock session earned by presenting WEB_ACTION_TOKEN on the report page. It authenticates against the token, so it needs actions enabled AND a token set — otherwise it fails closed (history denied to everyone; a startup warning notes the lockout). The report views (/ and /api/report) are never affected.

Config.Name: WEB_ACTION_REPORT_AUTH
Config.Description: Require the reclaim credential to view the report surface / and /index.html and /api/report (which expose media paths and sizes). Off keeps it LAN-readable (the default). When true, /api/report needs the X-Action-Token header and the browser routes need the unlock session; because / is the page you normally unlock through, its gated 403 page carries a no-JS token form (POST /actions/unlock) that mints the session and redirects back. Independent of WEB_ACTION_HISTORY_AUTH — set both to gate the entire read surface. Needs actions enabled AND a token set, or it fails closed (report denied to everyone; a startup warning notes the lockout).

Config.Name: WEB_ACTION_INLINE_SCRIPT
Config.Description: Add the self-contained inline enhancement scripts (each admitted by a fresh per-response script-src nonce, no external asset). Off (the default) keeps the strict no-script CSP. When true (and actions are enabled): the reclaim form gains select-all plus a live selected count and size (no fetch/XHR); and the in-flight report-regeneration status page live-polls GET /api/rescan and reflects completion without the no-JS full-page meta-refresh — that one page also gains a scoped connect-src self so the poll may fetch. Both degrade gracefully with JavaScript disabled (the form submits; the status page still meta-refreshes).
